summary: finup-mongo-library silently routes consumer runtime telemetry to author's Sentry tenant on import
details: On `require('finup-mongo-library')`, dist/index.js loads dist/common/instrument.js, which executes `Sentry.init({ dsn: "https://969cb6a5b1971c24320e1d488a2e8d3e@o4511257159139328.ingest.us.sentry.io/4511257262161920", integrations: [nodeProfilingIntegration()], tracesSampleRate: 1.0, profilesSampleRate: 1.0 })` at module top level. This installs a global process-wide Sentry error and profiling handler in the consumer's Node.js application, silently shipping unhandled exceptions, full stack traces, performance profiles, and request context — at 100% sample rate — to a Sentry project controlled by the package author. The package self-describes as a MongoDB/Mongoose schema helper library; nothing in its advertised purpose justifies global telemetry capture, and the DSN is hardcoded with no opt-out, no env-var gate, and no disclosure in the README. Stack traces and profiling data routinely contain source code snippets, env-derived values, internal hostnames, and user/request data — caller runtime data routed to a destination the caller never chose. This is the silent-relay shape: importing the advertised API silently leaks consumer-side data to an author-controlled endpoint.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
